paper_metadata.title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations
paper_metadata.abstract: Increasing model size when pretraining natural language representations often results in improved performance on downstream tasks. However, at some point further model increases become harder due to GPU/TPU memory limitations and longer training times. To address these problems, we present two parameter-reduction techniques to lower memory consumption and increase the training speed of BERT. Comprehensive empirical evidence shows that our proposed methods lead to models that scale much better compared to the original BERT. We also use a self-supervised loss that focuses on modeling inter-sentence coherence, and show it consistently helps downstream tasks with multi-sentence inputs. As a result, our best model establishes new state-of-the-art results on the GLUE, RACE, and \squad benchmarks while having fewer parameters compared to BERT-large. The code and the pretrained models are available at https://github.com/google-research/ALBERT.

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces two parameter-reduction techniques: factorized embedding parameterization (decomposing the large vocabulary embedding matrix into two smaller matrices) and cross-layer parameter sharing (preventing parameter growth with network depth). It also replaces BERT's Next Sentence Prediction (NSP) with Sentence-Order Prediction (SOP) to improve inter-sentence coherence modeling.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Same as BERT (BooksCorpus and English Wikipedia)
extraction_data.extension: Factorized embedding parameterization reduces embedding parameters from O(V × H) to O(V × E + E × H), and cross-layer parameter sharing stabilizes network parameters across layers.
extraction_data.research_problem: Scaling pre-trained language models efficiently by reducing memory consumption and training time while maintaining or improving performance on downstream tasks.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text classification, question answering, and other downstream NLP tasks
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT introduces parameter-reduction techniques (factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing) to lower memory consumption and increase training speed, alongside Sentence Order Prediction (SOP) as a self-supervised loss to improve inter-sentence coherence over Next Sentence Prediction (NSP).
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Same as BERT (BooksCorpus and English Wikipedia)
extraction_data.extension: Factorized embedding parameterization and cross-layer parameter sharing to reduce memory consumption and increase training speed without significantly impacting performance.
extraction_data.research_problem: Scaling pre-trained language models efficiently by reducing memory consumption and training time while maintaining or improving performance on downstream tasks.
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, reading comprehension, text classification, question answering
extraction_data.paper_title: ALBERT: A Lite BERT for Self-supervised Learning of Language Representations

extraction_data.innovation: ALBERT-xxlarge is the largest variant, achieving the best performance on benchmarks like GLUE (89.4), RACE (89.4), and SQuAD 2.0 (F1 92.2) while still having fewer parameters than BERT-large due to its parameter-efficient design.
